title: LEARNAKAN
description:
Learn Twi with our in-depth Twi language lessons!

Hi, my name is Stephen Awiba, known here as Tikya Yaw (a proud Thursday born :)). I started the LEARNAKAN project in 2016 with the aim of promoting the reading, writing, and speaking of the Akan language within and outside Ghana. The project, so far, comprises of:

1. our main website: https://learnakan.com
2. our online English-Twi; Twi-English dictionary: https://learnakandictionary.com
4. the Facebook page: LearnAkan
5. this YouTube channel.

Here, you will find Twi lessons on all the important aspects of the language, e.g. on Twi grammar, Twi vocabulary, Commonly-used Twi phrases, conversational Twi, Twi pronunciation, as well as general videos about Ghana, the Akan people and Akan culture. 

Nice to meet you! Akwaaba!

title: Hollywood Veil
description:
Hollywood Veil is a YouTube channel dedicated to uncovering the hidden side of Hollywood between 1960 and 1980—an era often regarded as one of the most transformative and captivating in film history. From the rise of New Hollywood to the shifting cultural landscape, the channel dives deep into the stories behind the stars, studios, and iconic productions that defined a generation.

Through carefully researched content, Hollywood Veil reveals behind-the-scenes moments, untold secrets, and lesser-known facts that shaped the industry. Each video explores the contrast between on-screen glamour and off-screen reality, offering viewers a richer understanding of classic cinema and the people who brought it to life.

If you are passionate about vintage films, intrigued by Hollywood history, or curious about the mysteries hidden behind the spotlight, Hollywood Veil invites you to step beyond the curtain and rediscover a legendary era.

title: Alma Film Festival
description:
Cultural Intelligence Engine + Deep Community Connection + Global Cinematic Agenda

The Alma Film Festival was created in response to structural gaps in the global film ecosystem as a next-generation destination and experience-based cultural event. Functioning as a cultural intelligence engine, bringing together filmmakers, scholars, technologists, artists, and audiences from across the Global South and its diasporas.

Rooted in scholarship, innovation, and deep audience engagement, Alma prioritizes fewer films with greater intentionality, creating space for dialogue, relationship-building, and long-term collaboration. Up to 80% of the program highlights Global South cinema across narrative, documentary, shorts, animation, experimental work, audio storytelling, and new media. 

Alma has cultivated a community of stakeholders in 51 cities in 35 countries.

title: Senorita Grey
description:
Hi Guys and Gals, 

I am Senorita Grey, you can call me Ms Grey. I am Ghanaian and I have a passion for acting.
The hurdles of life have played a part in me suppressing that burning desire for a number of years now but it has resurfaced again.  

Living in the UK, but going back to my roots to give all I have to establish myself on the Ghallywood/Nollywood acting scene. A lot of sacrifice will be made and my life here in UK will effectively be put on hold but I believe in my heart that it will be more than worth it.

So I created this channel to document my journey, from UK to Ghallywood. I will be posting regularly about the process, the difficulties, the exciting bits, my fears and apprehensions. I also want to share with you the reality that I will discover and compare it to the assumptions that many people, including myself, hold about the Ghanaian/African movie industry. 

So jump aboard, subscribe and stay tuned! There's plenty to come.

title: Hollywood East - Topic
description:
Hollywood East

He started from the bottom and now he’s…well…he’s still there…but he’s lovin’ every minute of it.

A dynamically versatile artist with a genuine perspective all his own, Hollywood East knows exactly what it takes to get by with nothing at all and appreciate both the good & bad in life. 

He grew to accept & see life through a different lens, as if it were a movie…to accept the light & the dark, the happy & sad, all as part of one incredible experience within this starring role.  Driven by the determination to create his own perfect world for himself & his family to live in – Hollywood East focused hard on his goals, and turned to music as the ultimate vehicle to get there.  Never willing to settle, never complacent, always hustling, always grindin’ – the only option Hollywood East has ever considered is success.  With that, he’d have all the opportunities he needs to create that better world.
